channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: California sues to stop Trump from imposing sweeping tariffs
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California Gov. Gavin Newsom sued the Trump administration on Wednesday, challenging the president's authority to impose sweeping tariffs that have set off a global trade war.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Behind the story of the decades-long journey of xenotransplantation
item.description: In this episode of The Story Behind the AP Story, we hear from Lauran Neergaard and Shelby Lum, who have been following the journey of animal to human transplants, or xenotransplants, over the last few years.
item.dc:creator: Haya Panjwani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Arson attack probe at Pennsylvania governor's mansion looking into suspect's hatred of Josh Shapiro
item.description: HERSHEY, Pa. (AP) — Authorities investigating why a man set fire to Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro’s official residence are probing whether the suspect was motivated by the Democrat's Jewish faith or positions on Israel’s war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Is Zoom down? Thousands report issues accessing software
item.description: Thousands of Zoom users reported issues accessing virtual meetings Wednesday, with the site confirming shortly after noon it was investigating the issue.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Student accused in Dallas school shooting walked toward group in hall and opened fire, injuring 4
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — The student accused of injuring four in a shooting at a Dallas high school was let into the building through an unsecured door and then walked down a hallway toward a group of students, opening fire on them and then appearing to take a point-blank shot at one, according to an arrest
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: AmeriCorps members who respond to disasters and help nonprofits are let go in DOGE cuts
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Young volunteers who respond to natural disasters and help with community projects across the U.S. have been discharged as a result of the Trump administration 's campaign to shrink government workforce and services.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wall Street banker washes up dead on paradise beach weeks after disappearing on vacation
item.description: The family of a missing New York banker confirmed that the remains that washed ashore on a beach in Spain belong to the 37-year-old, who had vanished nearly a month ago during vacation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Watch: Crews rescue stranded hiker 'death gripping' cliff on California trail
item.description: “She was able to send an emergency text to 911 via her Garmin-type emergency communications device,” the Instagram post said.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, NewsNation

item.title: Teens slashed adults who told them to be quiet during movie, police say
item.description: According to police, the boys, who were between the ages of 14 and 16, got upset after they were told to be quiet during a movie.
item.dc:creator: Melanie DaSilva, NewsNation

item.title: Biden, Obama-appointed judges oversee bulk of Trump cases
item.description: The Gavel is The Hill's courts newsletter. Sign up by clicking here or adding your email in the box below.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: One dead in multi-boat crash during Major League Fishing tournament in Alabama
item.description: A fatal boat crash during a Major League Fishing tournament in Alabama is currently under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Austin Franklin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Maryland Sen. Van Hollen meets with El Salvador's vice president in push for prisoner's release
item.description: Maryland Sen. Chris Van Hollen traveled to El Salvador on Wednesday and met with the country’s vice president to push for the release of Kilmar Abrego Garcia, a man who was sent there by the Trump administration in March despite an immigration court order preventing his deportation.
item.dc:creator: Mary Clare Jalonick And Matthew Brown, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Gambian ex-soldier convicted at US trial of torturing suspected backers of a failed 2006 coup
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A former member of Gambia's military was convicted in federal court Tuesday of torturing five people accused of involvement in a failed coup against the West African country's longtime dictator nearly 20 years ago, capping a rare prosecution in the United States for torture committed a
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Santa Fe sheriff releases body camera footage of Gene Hackman investigation
item.description: Newly-released body camera footage shows the confusion and questions investigators had after actor Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa were found dead in their home.
item.dc:creator: Marilyn Upchurch, Fox 5

item.title: Man arrested outside UnitedHealthcare is now charged with firearms counts and threatening violence
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A man who was arrested outside the headquarters of UnitedHealthcare had a history of mental health issues and threatened to shoot up corporate facilities, according to charges filed Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Make-A-Wish Missouri & Kansas grants boy's wish to meet Patrick Mahomes
item.description: Ben got the surprise of a lifetime: the chance to meet his football hero, Patrick Mahomes, thanks to Make-A-Wish Missouri & Kansas.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Pettey, NewsNation

item.title: Georgia man arrested over alleged threats to kill Tulsi Gabbard
item.description: A Georgia man was arrested after federal authorities said he made numerous death threats against Director of National Intelligence (DNI) Tulsi Gabbard, the Justice Department announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Go inside the factory where Peeps are made
item.description: BETHLEHEM, Pa. (AP) — Love them or hate them, those marshmallow Peeps that come in blindingly bright colors and an array of flavors are inescapable around the Easter holiday.
item.dc:creator: TASSANEE VEJPONGSA and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump not expected to attend White House Correspondents' dinner
item.description: President Donald Trump is not expected to attend next weekend's White House Correspondents' dinner, The Hill confirmed on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KRON4

item.title: Why every MLB player will wear the same number Tuesday
item.description: Every MLB player, regardless of team or position, will be wearing the exact same jersey number on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: McDonald's teases return of Snack Wraps
item.description: McDonald’s may be one step closer to reintroducing its discontinued Snack Wraps, teasing a cryptic return date on social media Tuesday. On X, the company posted “snack wraps 0x.14.2025,” revealing the date the item will return but not the month.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Suspect in arson attacks at Tesla showroom, New Mexico GOP headquarters facing 40 years in prison
item.description: Federal charges have been filed against New Mexico man Jamison Wagner, 40, in connection to the arson attacks at the Tesla Albuquerque Showroom and the Republican Party of New Mexico (RPNM) headquarters.
item.dc:creator: Audrey Claire Davis, KRON4

item.title: Watch: Elephants at San Diego park form protective circle during earthquake
item.description: Watch: Elephants at the San Diego Zoo Safari Park surrounded the younger elephants in a protective "alert circle" when a 5.2 magnitude earthquake struck the region Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, ABC 4

item.title: IRS tax deadline extended in multiple states: What to know
item.description: Residents in all or parts of 13 states can file for an extension to allow disaster-affected areas more time to submit their taxes.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, WGN 9

item.title: Trump administration freezes $2.2 billion in grants to Harvard over campus activism
item.description: The federal government says it’s freezing more than $2.2 billion in grants and $60 million in contracts to Harvard University, since the institution said Monday it won’t comply with the Trump administration’s demands to limit activism on campus.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Video shows mother pick up baby and run during Southern California earthquake
item.description: A quick-thinking San Diego County mother saved her baby from a 5.2 magnitude earthquake in eastern San Diego County.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Immigration judges sue over mass firings at DOJ
item.description: A group of recently appointed immigration judges who were fired by mass layouts have filed a lawsuit against the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Judge denies bail for man accused of setting fire at Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro's residence
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A man who allegedly scaled an iron security fence in the middle of the night, eluded police and set fire to the Pennsylvania governor’s mansion was denied bail Monday as he faced charges including attempted homicide, terrorism and arson.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO and MARC LEVY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Stocks rally worldwide after Trump eases some of his tariffs on electronics, for now
item.description: Stocks rose worldwide Monday after President Donald Trump relaxed some of his tariffs, for now at least, and as stress from within the U.S. bond market seems to be easing.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Reported intruder arrested near UnitedHealthcare headquarters months after CEO's killing
item.description: Police in the Minneapolis suburb of Minnetonka said the suspect was arrested outside of the United Healthcare corporate campus.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Boxing legend George Foreman remembered for his love of family, faith and cheeseburgers
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — George Foreman was remembered Monday in a memorial service in his hometown of Houston for his legendary boxing career as well as for his love of God, family, horses and cheeseburgers and for his desire to help his fellow man. “He preached love all the time.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: One US college student released after two detained in Denmark for alleged Uber incident
item.description: One of the two U.S. college students detained in Denmark over an alleged assault following an Uber ride has been released, according to a statement from his family.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Mathis, WGN 9

item.title: Trump says he's open to sending violent criminals who are US citizens to El Salvador prison
item.description: President Trump on Monday indicated he would be open to sending American citizens who are violent criminals to El Salvador to be held in a notorious prison there, telling reporters his administration is studying law on the matter.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, ABC 4
